heading: Provision and acceptance of support by Librarian of Congress
label: 151707 Provision and acceptance of support by Librarian of Congress
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)151707(a)36:5704., , §§ 205, 206, .Oct. 11, 1996Pub. L. 104–285, title II110 Stat. 3385151707(b)36:5705.In subsection (b), the words “chapters 33 and 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5 and related regulations” are substituted for “the civil service classification laws, rules, or regulations” for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the United States Code.

children.text: The Librarian of Congress may provide personnel, facilities, and other administrative services to the corporation. Administrative services may include reimbursement of expenses under , at rates not exceeding the applicable per diem rates for the United States Government.section 151703(f) of this title

The corporation shall reimburse the Librarian for support provided under paragraph (1) of this subsection. Amounts reimbursed shall be deposited in the Treasury to the credit of the appropriations then current and chargeable for the cost of providing the support.

children.heading: .—Acceptance by Librarian
children.label: b .—Acceptance by Librarian
children.text: The Librarian may accept, without regard to chapters 33 and 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5 and related regulations, the services of the corporation and its directors, officers, and employees as volunteers in performing functions authorized under this chapter, without compensation from the Library of Congress.
